title: Burgers conservative update preserves total under periodic flux

source.citation: Randall J. LeVeque, Finite Volume Methods for Hyperbolic Problems, Cambridge University Press, 2002, Sec. 11.3, p. 208 and Ch. 4, pp. 64-65.
source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: If the Burgers flux satisfies the periodic boundary condition, then the finite total is preserved by the Burgers conservative update.
informal.latex: If \(F_n=F_0\) as a periodic-boundary condition, then \(\sum_i u_i^+=\sum_i u_i\).
assumptions: scale is a real update scale
u is a real-valued grid state on natural-number indices
n is a natural-number range length
informal.expected_conclusion: Periodic Burgers flux implies total preservation for the Burgers update.

declarations.name: burgersConservativeUpdate
declarations.statement: def burgersConservativeUpdate (scale : Real) (u : Nat -> Real) (i : Nat) : Real := conservativeUpdate scale u (burgersFlux u) i

declarations.name: totalOn_burgersConservativeUpdate_of_periodicFlux
declarations.statement: theorem totalOn_burgersConservativeUpdate_of_periodicFlux (scale : Real) (u : Nat -> Real) (n : Nat) (hflux : periodicBoundary (burgersFlux u) n) : totalOn (fun i => burgersConservativeUpdate scale u i) n = totalOn u n

tasks.input: Translate this Burgers conservative finite-difference theorem into Lean 4: If the Burgers flux satisfies the periodic boundary condition, then the finite total is preserved by the Burgers conservative update.
tasks.target: theorem totalOn_burgersConservativeUpdate_of_periodicFlux (scale : Real) (u : Nat -> Real) (n : Nat) (hflux : periodicBoundary (burgersFlux u) n) : totalOn (fun i => burgersConservativeUpdate scale u i) n = totalOn u n
